experiments.researchQuestion: Combining common ad sets into larger audiences in a Facebook campaign will increase donor conversion and decrease cost per donor.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/02/2021 - 02/28/2021
The Facebook campaigns for the Missionary Oblates had multiple ad sets for similar audiences. For instance, they Catholic online, Knights of Columbus, Catholic and Proud, were all separate ad sets in Facebook, but are all Catholic audiences. In Facebook it’s more efficient to have ad sets with a larger reach because Facebook is more likely to spend money in a larger ad set. So we hypothesized that combining those smaller ad sets into one big Catholic ad set would increase efficiency and lower cost per donor. We tested a campaign with their broken out ad sets against a campaign with combined ad set of a common audience.
experiments.keyLearnings: As the results show, Facebook had to reach fewer people with combined ad sets. It did increase donor acquisition by 18%, but it did not reach validity, with only 84% level of confidence. However, this did not decrease the cost per donor. In fact, the cost per donor was 3% higher with the test campaign that combined audiences in the ad set. And it decreased the ROI by 9%. We will need to look at new ways to test and optimize Facebook audiences.

experiments.title: How adding a donate button increases donor conversion rates for Oblates
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a donate button for website visitors will achieve increased donor conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 01/31/2022 - 03/04/2022
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate had a website where the donate call to action from the homepage as part of the header navigation menu and not a button as most non-profit websites have. As they are in the process of getting a new redesign, we wanted to test whether creating a donation button would increase donor conversion rates.
experiments.keyLearnings:
Adding in a donate button increased donor conversion rates by 15.5% overall, while the increase for new visitors was over 27%.

Observations of the impact on other visitor segments in the experiment:
Organic Search visitors had a 31.9% increase in donations with an 86% level of confidence.
 	New visitors had a 27.2% increase in donations with a 94% level of confidence.
 	35-44 visitors had a 105.5% increase in donations with an 89% level of confidence.
 	18-24 visitors had a 168.4% increase in donations with a 92% level of confidence.

experiments.title: How does a homepage takeover affect year end donations and revenue?
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Sending direct homepage traffic a calendar year end giving page will increase year end donations.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/30/2020 - 12/31/2020
During their calendar year end campaign, on December 30th and 31st, we tested redirecting 50% of the Oblates direct homepage traffic to the calendar year end donation page. The hypothesis was that most people who come to a page on that day will be interested in giving, or more likely to give.
experiments.keyLearnings:
This test saw an 11% increase in donations, which resulted in 45 more gifts. We recommend including this in calendar year end tactics in the coming year.

Observations of the impact on other visitor segments in the experiment:
Mobile visitors had a 19.8% increase in donations with a 95% level of confidence.
 	Email visitors had a 16.8% increase in donations with a 99% level of confidence.
 	Returning visitors had a 18.8% increase in donations with a 96% level of confidence.

experiments.title: Does including a lapsed donor’s last gift information increase donor reactivation?
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does simply including a lapsed donor’s last gift date gently nudge them to give again?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 10/13/2020
During their Fall Campaign, Oblates wanted to reach out to lapsed donors who had an active email. In order to increase reactivation, we tried a test that has proven successful with other organizations. One version was the same copy as the other campaigns. The test version included the last gift date and last gift amount for each lapsed donor. The purpose was to remind them that it had been some time since they last gave. We only included donors who had not given in the last 5 years. This was tested over a series of two emails, one final day campaign email and then a forward of that email from the primary signer’s secretary.
experiments.keyLearnings: Including their giving amount does seem to have prompted the lapsed donors to give again. However, as there are only three gifts in this experiment, we recommend further testing and perhaps with donors that a deeper lapsed.

experiments.title: How adding an item instead of a free book increases donations
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Could we get more donations by providing a more appealing item as an incentive if they donate $25 or more.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/14/2019 - 06/15/2019
We wanted to know if providing an item such as a rosary would be perceived as more valuable than a book and increase donations. We tested this with a Facebook Ad and an Email that lead people to one of these donation pages.
experiments.keyLearnings: If we can provide an item that is perceived as more valuable we can increase the amount of donations. We can use this going forward for other Holidays like Christmas. Instead of offering a free book we can provide a certain item for $25 and increase donations.

experiments.title: How moving Lourdes water to a one page donation increased donations
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We wanted to learn if a one step checkout process would help increase donations for Lourdes Water.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/20/2019 - 10/27/2019
We get a lot of traffic to make a donation for Lourdes Water. It’s our most popular item, so we wanted to try to find a way people could make a donation easier.
experiments.keyLearnings: It increased donations and conversion rate, but the revenue stayed about the same. For our longer checkout process we have an area that asks them to make an additional donation. We found that this is why there was less donations, but similar amount in revenue. We are going to move forward using the one step process for the Lourdes Water, but continue to test different ways to increase the average gifts.

experiments.title: How moving Lourdes water to a one page donation increased donations
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We wanted to learn if a one step checkout process would help increase donations for Lourdes Water.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 09/20/2019 - 10/27/2019
We get a lot of traffic to make a donation for Lourdes Water. It’s our most popular item, so we wanted to try to find a way people could make a donation easier.
experiments.keyLearnings: It increased donations and conversion rate, but the revenue stayed about the same. For our longer checkout process we have an area that asks them to make an additional donation. We found that this is why there was less donations, but similar amount in revenue. We are going to move forward using the one step process for the Lourdes Water, but continue to test different ways to increase the average gifts.

experiments.title: How removing fields that aren’t necessary on an acquisition form impacts name conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Would a simplified form increase name conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/24/2018 - 06/08/2018
The Missionary Oblates offer a free online course called Celebrating Mercy. They hypothesized that simplifying the acquisition form might increase name conversion for the course. The control had additional fields on it that we hypothesized weren’t necessary. To test this, we streamlined the form and removed the address fields (address, city, state, zip code). All other elements remained the same.
experiments.keyLearnings: By simplifying the form we increase name conversion by 8%. This experiment didn’t validate, but the results we were seeing were directional with a 87.5% confidence. The address fields aren’t required for a person to take the course. These additional fields were creating friction to the conversion process and by removing them we were able to capture more people and get them enrolled in the course.

experiments.title: How the copy tone of an ad impacts conversion.
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Which ad would acquire more email addresses?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/04/2017 - 04/18/2017
The Missionary Oblates offered a special Easter Novena to people through paid Facebook ads. They wanted to test the motivation of the end-user by testing the tone of the copy and how they presented the offer. The control ad presented the offer early on in the copy and had a more uplifting tone with an invitation to get the offer. The treatment talked about the importance of Easter and encouraged people to get the Novena.
experiments.keyLearnings: This test didn’t quite validate and there was only a slight difference between the control and the treatment. The initial data showed the ad with a more uplifting tone that presented the offer earlier in the copy had a 8% increase in acquisition. We recommend that a more radical test be done using some of these same concepts from the winning variant in order to validate the experiment in the future.

experiments.title: How presenting a relevant premium offer on a page with high volumes of traffic can impact donor conversion.
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: How would adding a donation appeal impact donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 07/17/2017 - 09/05/2017
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate get significant traffic to their Daily Prayers webpage. People are coming to this webpage from a daily email. Knowing the significant amount of traffic the page gets, we hypothesized we might be able to convert some of these web visitors into donors. We tested adding a donation opportunity on the page with a relevant premium offer. We split the traffic and tested the two variables.
experiments.keyLearnings: While the results weren’t statistically valid (83.5%), we were able to increase donor conversion by 23.2%. What this shows us is that we can convert high volumes highly engaged traffic into donors when we present them with a donation opportunity with a relevant premium offer.

experiments.title: How year-end specific copy affects main donation page conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does year-end specific copy increase donations on a main donation page?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 12/22/2017 - 01/02/2018
The Missionary Oblates were running a test on their main donation page for year-end. They tested year-end specific copy against their year-round main donation page. They also removed an image and made the headline bigger.
experiments.keyLearnings: Although the experiment did not validate, we saw a trend upwards with the year-end specific treatment, with a 12% increase in donations and a significantly higher average gift with the treatment copy. This is likely due to the urgency in the copy along with an automatically more urgent page due to the timing of the test (highest fundraising time of the year).

experiments.title: How adding a statement of giving impacts lapsed reactivation rates in an email
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a donor statement for lapsed donors will achieve a difference in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/28/2022 - 11/28/2022
Giving Tuesday is generally a big day of giving for many non-profits. For the Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate and their early Giving Tuesday email targeting lapsed donors, we wanted to see whether including a ‘statement’ of their donation history would help increase lapsed reactivation rates. This email was deployed on 11/28, with the only difference between the control and the treatment being the addition of a statement at the top of the email of the donor’s last gift date, amount, etc.
Our hypothesis was that by adding this statement at the top, it would serve as a visual reminder for lapsed donors of how long it had been since their last gift to the Oblates.
experiments.keyLearnings:
With the addition of a donor statement at the top of the email, the treatment version had a 39% higher conversion rate than the control. However, given the lower number of donations total for this lapsed audience, this experiment only had a 43% level of confidence.

Further testing throughout the year-end campaign is warranted to see if a long-term test would validate.

experiments.title: How offering a survey about cards changed response rates
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that asking for themed responses for survey responders will achieve change the response rate.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 10/15/2021 - 10/29/2021
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. They offer physical cards during Eastern and Mother’s Day; for this experiment, we wanted to survey their email audience to see whether people would respond more for an Easter ask versus a Mother’s Day ask.
experiments.keyLearnings:
Both the Mother's Day control and the Easter treatment had essentially the same click-through rates to the survey, with only a 1.3% decline with the Easter treatment version, for a 33.6% level of confidence.

Since the results of this experiment were so close, this experiment will be tweaked and re-launched for next year.

experiments.title: How adding COVID-19 language to a donation page impacted donor conversion results
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding COVID-19 language for donors will achieve a chance in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/08/2020 - 06/04/2020
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. As COVID-19 rapidly spread across the globe, we launched an experiment to see whether adding COVID-19 language to a donation form would make an impact on donation conversions.
experiments.keyLearnings:
With the pandemic rapidly spreading across the globe, our hypothesis was that by adding in COVID language more people would end up donating. However, there was a slight decrease in conversion rates of 1.3% on the COVID treatment page.

We believe that this was due to the fact that the pandemic, and it's impact on the people that the Oblates serve, was not yet widely known.

experiments.title: How adding a sticky bar to the homepage during a fiscal year-end campaign impacted donor conversion rates
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that individualizing copy for donors will achieve a change in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/10/2021 - 07/02/2021
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. One of their standard high urgency campaigns takes place in June, as their fiscal year comes to a close.
In 2021, we wanted to test whether changing the language on the sticky bar to a more individualized approach–where by donating you are helping specific members of society–rather than a more generic ask, would have an impact on conversion rates.
experiments.keyLearnings: The results of this experiment were negligible, with only a 1.5% change in conversion rates from the control to the individualized copy version. With only a 6.4% level of confidence, this experiment needs to be re-run for a longer time period, during a campaign where there will potentially be more conversions to provide a valid test result.

experiments.title: How calling out the steps to take to make a donation impacted donor conversion rates.
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate

rows.name: Steps to take to make a donation

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding visual ‘steps’ for donors will achieve a change in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/08/2020 - 09/09/2020
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. As part of one of their acquisition offers which offered a download of a Lift the Spirit eBook, we wanted to test whether adding a visual call out for the different parts of a donation form impacted conversion results.
In this test, we added ‘Step 1, step 2, etc’ to each section of the donation form (gift array, billing info, etc) to test whether a guided process made an impact.
experiments.keyLearnings: Adding in the 'steps' for a donor to follow during their donation process led to a decrease in conversion rates of 16.7%. While the sample size for this experiment is not large--due to it coming from an acquisition experiment--there was a 68% level of confidence, indicating that continued testing and refinement would be needed in the future.

experiments.title: How increasing the suggested ask amount for a premium offer during year-end impacted revenue
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that changing the suggested ask for donors will achieve a change in revenue.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/20/2021 - 01/04/2022
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. One of their most popular offers during their end-of-year campaign is to donate a specific amount and have 4 advent candles lit for your intentions at the Shrine of Our Lady of the Snows.
Since year-end is where the majority of online revenue comes in for the Oblates, we wanted to test whether increase the ask for the Advent offer from $50 to $75 would make an impact on revenue from the campaign.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The results of this experiment were interesting. Overall, there was a decline in revenue per visitor of 9.5% with a 73% level of confidence. That means that, despite a higher average gift on the $75 experiment version, total revenue was less with the higher ask.

However, the results also varied by the kind of donor that landed on the page. New visitors had a decrease of 33% in revenue with a 96% level of confidence; our hypothesis was that the $75 donation was just too high for new donors.

The story for returning visitors was different, however, with a 28.6% increase in revenue and a 89% level of confidence. Those that knew the work of the Oblates, and were interested in giving again to have advent candles lit for their intentions, were willing to donate at the higher ask.

This is an experiment we will continue to refine in future campaigns.

experiments.title: How adding a visual call-out to for a monthly gift ask impacted conversion rates.
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a visual monthly call out for potential donors will achieve a change in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/01/2022 - 05/16/2022
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. The recruitment of monthly donors makes up a large part of their digital program; new monthly donors are offered the opportunity to have a candle lit at the shrine of our Lady of the Snows every month.
For this experiment, which we ran during the Easter season, we wanted to test whether having a visual call-out for monthly gifts made an impact on monthly donor sign-ups.
experiments.keyLearnings:
For this experiment, there was a decrease in the number of monthly gifts that came in during this testing period of 28%, with a 78% level of confidence. While the total number of gifts is just barely over the number we like to have to validate an experiment (50), there were a couple of differences in the two forms that contributed to mixed testing results.
On the control version, there is a check box one needs to select in order to make your gift become monthly; for the treatment version we not only introduced the visual call out, but also added in a tabbed form. We believe that this contributed to the change in conversion rates.
For future tests, we will try and isolate just a single change on the form to get a cleaner test result.

experiments.title: How adding a ‘most popular’ treatment to a specific gift amount impacted revenue.
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that highlighting a most popular gift amount for potential donors will achieve a change in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/01/2022 - 04/07/2022
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. As part of the run-up to their Easter fundraising campaign, we wanted to test whether adding a graphic treatment for the ‘most popular’ amount would have an impact on conversion rates and average gift.
experiments.keyLearnings: This experiment resulted in a slight increase in conversion rate with the most popular treatment option, as well as a slight increase in average gift. However, since it did not reach the level of confidence needed for a valid test result nor can provide any directional results, we will need to continue testing this approach to see if it has a long-term impact on donor behavior.

experiments.title: How adding a ‘handwritten’ call out for monthly donations impacted donation conversion rates
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate

rows.name: Add handwritten note focusing on monthly donations

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a graphic call out for potential monthly donors will achieve a change in conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/09/2021 - 04/22/2021
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. The recruitment of monthly donors makes up a large part of their digital program; new monthly donors are offered the opportunity to have a candle lit at the shrine of our Lady of the Snows every month.
For this experiment we tested whether a graphic call-out, in the form of some ‘handwritten’ text, focusing around the impact monthly donations would make, had an impact on monthly donor sign-ups.
experiments.keyLearnings: By adding in the 'handwritten' font emphasizing what an impact a monthly gift could make, there was a 21% increase in the number of new monthly donations converted on this experiment. While the level of confidence was only 56% due to the lower volume of recurring gifts, this result did indicated that further testing and refinement was needed.

experiments.title: How adding a $5 minimum gift requirement impacts donor conversion rates
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that adding a minimum donation amount for donors will achieve a change in conversion rates..
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/20/2020 - 04/17/2020
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. One of their most popular digital acquisition offers is the Our Lady of Lourdes digital novena.
Traditionally, there was a premium associated with this offer in an effort to get ebook sign-ups to donate, with an offer of a vial of water from the sacred Lourdes grotto in France as a thank for a donor’s gift. For this experiment, we added a $5 minimum donation to the premium ask to see whether it would have an impact on donor conversion rates.
experiments.keyLearnings:
Adding in the $5 minimum gift, with language that made it clear the minimum was to cover shipping and handling costs, had a slight increase of 8% in terms of revenue per visitor, with a 72% level of confidence. Moreover, there was a 28% increase in overall donation conversion rates with a 98% level of confidence.

Our hypothesis is that by positioning the minimum gift requirement as a way to make sure the donor's donation is maximized (instead of spent on shipping and handling) led donors to be more generous with their gifts.

This kind of language merits continued testing throughout premium acquisition offers for the Oblates.

experiments.title: How splitting out a monthly donate button impacted monthly donor donations
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that calling attention to a monthly donor program for potential donors will achieve a change in donor conversion rates.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/18/2022 - 05/16/2022
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate serve poor and needy people in the United States and more than 70 countries around the world. The recruitment of monthly donors makes up a large part of their digital program; new monthly donors are offered the opportunity to have a candle lit at the shrine of our Lady of the Snows every month.
For this experiment, which we ran during the Easter season, we wanted to test whether splitting out a donate and monthly-donate-specific button would have any impact on new monthly donor conversions.
experiments.keyLearnings:
This experiment had a 23% increase in the number of new monthly donors who were converted using the monthly call out button, but only had a 46% level of confidence given the lower total number of new monthly donors that came in during that time period.
Despite this experiment running during what is a traditionally a high-impact giving season (Easter), the number of donations was not enough to give us a valid result.
Nevertheless, this is a concept that we will continue testing on the Oblate's main website, especially given the importance of new monthly donors on their program overall.

experiments.title: How Identity Language affects donor conversion on a fiscal year end donation page
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Bringing clarity to who the donation helps will increase donor conversion.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/29/2021 - 07/09/2021
During the Oblates Fiscal Year End campaign, we wanted to test identity language on different elements of the campaign. We tested this language on the donation page during the last two days of the campaign.
experiments.keyLearnings: While there was a distinct increase in giving from the email test, the donation page did not show as clear an impact. Our hypothesis is that the motivation was higher for all donation page visitors than for the people who opened the email. Further testing needs to be done for how we can use this strategy in the future.

experiments.title: How increasing the force of the value proposition and removing elements of friction impact donor conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Would strengthening the value proposition on the primary donation page increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 01/14/2019 - 03/29/2019
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate’s recently changed the kind of donation platform they were on and saw an increase in donor conversion just by improving the donor experience with the new platform. Now, they wanted to test the messaging to see if they could increase donor motivation even more. To do this, they added a headline that reinforced the value proposition of the page, strengthened the messaging, removed elements of copy that didn’t enhance the value proposition and added a clear call-to-action.
experiments.keyLearnings: After running the experiment for more than two months, we saw no difference in donor conversion. This can be attributed to the overall low donor conversion on the page. We propose testing other elements on the page that might increase overall revenue rather than increase donor conversion. The next experiment we will run in an effort to do this is testing around the suggested gift array.

experiments.title: How lowering the donation ask for the Daily Prayer Book impacts transactions and revenue
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will lowering the ask amount to $10 increase donations and revenue?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 08/14/2019 - 11/15/2019
We have a page that offers a Daily Prayer book for $25. We thought that this could be a little to high.
experiments.keyLearnings: There was no difference in the amount of donations when adding a lower amount. The control had an Avg. Order Value of $24.09 with $2,072 Revenue. The treatment had an Avg. Order Value of $20.91 with $1,798 Revenue.

experiments.title: How changing the sender name from Fr. David P. Uribe, OMI to Fr. David increases open rates for Daily Prayer
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will changing the name from Fr. David P. Uribe, OMI to Fr. David increase open rates?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 10/31/2019
We’ve previously tested the sender name for other emails and found that using Fr. David increased open rates, but we wanted to see if it would do the same for our Daily Prayer that we send out every morning.
experiments.keyLearnings: We found that using Fr. David did increase open rates, but not as much as our regular email did. We think because these recipients are so used to seeing the subject line Daily Prayer every morning that the sender name might not be as important to them.

experiments.title: How changing Fr. David P. Uribe, OMI to just Fr. David increases open rates for Daily Prayer
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will changing the name from Fr. David P. Uribe, OMI to Fr. David increase open rates?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 10/31/2019
We’ve previously tested the sender name for other emails and found that using Fr. David increased open rates, but we wanted to see if it would do the same for our Daily Prayer that we send out every morning.
experiments.keyLearnings: We found that using Fr. David did increase open rates, but not as much as our regular email did. We think because these recipients are so used to seeing the subject line Daily Prayer every morning that the sender name might not be as important to them.

experiments.title: How removing the banner of Evangelize emails improves clicks
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: We wanted to increase clicks with our Evangelize emails.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 09/27/2019
We tested this with our monthly Evangelize emails. We believed that people may see our monthly newsletter banner and immediately lose interest and not scroll down to the links.
experiments.keyLearnings: We found that this experiment had no impact on people clicking on the links. We will continue to for ways to improve our monthly newsletter emails.

experiments.title: How switching the email subject line to place the words gift and prayer first increases opens
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate

rows.name: Enroll a Loved One Today - Share the Gift of Prayer
Share the Gift of Prayer - Enroll a Loved One Today

experiments.researchQuestion: The question that we we’re wanting to answer is if this worked for every email. Would people be more willing to click if they noticed gift and prayer first in the subject line or would they still click on the email if that was towards the end of the subject line.
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 05/01/2019
We tested this through Mailchimp. We’ve noticed in previous tests and throughout our website analytics that most of the donors respond more when they see the words gift or prayer.
experiments.keyLearnings: The key takeaway is that the results were almost completely identical. This could be because we also had words like Loved Ones at the beginning of the email that also usually generates a lot of interest with our audience. Moving forward when running a test like this we will have to make sure there is no words that could make an impact emotionally like Loved Ones is.

experiments.title: How targeting direct mail prospects with digital advertising affected direct mail revenue
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does targeting direct mail prospects with branding ads and content offers via Facebook ads increase direct mail revenue?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/17/2019 - 12/31/2018
Missionary Oblates had significant investments in their direct mail program and wanted to see if they could optimize the return by investing in digital marketing that would target these prospects in the time period that they received a direct mail letter.
They created a control audience that was excluded from seeing any ads, and a treatment audience that would see a rotation of brand and eBook offering ads (but no donation-centric ads) for a two week period before the mail piece hit mailboxes and for two weeks after, the prime time in which a donor would respond. They optimized the ads for reach—aiming to show ads to as large a percentage of the target group as possible. The goal of this test was to lift direct mail revenue—not to add an additional segment of online revenue.
experiments.keyLearnings: This experiment was not valid. We’d like to test this again at a different time of year, when the motivation of donors isn’t necessarily as high as it is at year end.

experiments.title: How strengthening the value proposition on the primary donation page impacts donor conversion?
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Would strengthening the value proposition on the primary donation page increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 01/14/2019 - 03/29/2019
The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate’s recently changed the kind of donation platform they were on and saw an increase in donor conversion just by improving the donor experience with the new platform. Now, they wanted to test the messaging to see if they could increase donor motivation even more. To do this, they added a headline that reinforced the value proposition of the page, strengthened the messaging, removed elements of copy that didn’t enhance the value proposition and added a clear call-to-action.
experiments.keyLearnings: After running the experiment for more than two months, we saw no difference in donor conversion. This can be attributed to the overall low donor conversion on the page. We propose testing other elements on the page that might increase overall revenue rather than increase donor conversion. The next experiment we will run in an effort to do this is testing around the suggested gift array.

experiments.title: How using a button instead of a raw link impacts conversion in a cultivation email
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Would a button instead of a raw link increase clicks in a cultivation email?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 01/31/2019
The Missionary Oblates had tested into using raw links for the hyperlinks in their email communication. This finding was over a year ago and they have continued to use raw links in all of their emails since then. As they were sending a cultivation email to their email file they hypothesized that maybe their file had grown used to the raw link and it wouldn’t have the same impact as it had when they had originally tested it. They developed a treatment that used a button instead of a raw link and split their file in half to see the impact on clicks.
experiments.keyLearnings: We saw a directional lift of 5% from the treatment that had the button instead of the raw link. Because of the volume of emails that the Missionary Oblates send, we’d recommend testing this approach again in an effort to validate the approach and track conversions all the way through. We ultimately want to see the impact this kind of experiment has not only on clicks, but donations as well since the cultivation email offered people a free content offer that had a donation ask on the end of it.

experiments.title: How streamlining an acquisition page affects course signups
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does a more streamlined, value-focused acquisition page increase conversions on an online course?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/22/2018 - 06/08/2018
The Missionary Oblates were offering a free online course called Finding Your Prophetic Voice, and wanted to increase conversion on the landing page. To do this, they streamlined the page and used more value-focused body copy, along with a headline that better explained the value of the course to the taker. They also included testimonials from people who had taken another Oblates course to further explain the value.
experiments.keyLearnings: This experiment was not statistically valid, and will require more testing to identify specific learnings.

experiments.title: How a radical redesign of a donation page affects conversion rate
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does a redesigned donation page that removes header content increase conversion rate?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 07/03/2018
The Missionary Oblates were running a high-urgency fiscal year end campaign and wanted to test whether the format of the donation page was causing friction for people coming to the page and giving them decision anxiety. They proposed a radical redesign that removed all other links from the header and top of the page and streamlined the form and body copy. The copy was the same.
experiments.keyLearnings: The experiment was not statistically valid, but we did see a directional lift of 6% with the redesigned page. This concept would be good to test with additional campaigns along with other elements of the page to produce a valid lift.

experiments.title: How adding value proposition and removing friction on a donation page affects conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does removing friction and adding value proposition increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/05/2018 - 04/13/2018
The Missionary Oblates wanted to increase donations on their Mother’s Day premium gift pages. They wanted to test if streamlining the page by removing additional ways to leave the page (a back button) as well as adding value proposition to the copy would increase donations.
experiments.keyLearnings: The treatment decreased donor conversion rate by 32%. The motivation of people coming to this page is relatively high and it seems that the added value prop copy actually added friction to the conversion process rather than motivate more people to convert. Although this experiment wasn’t statistically valid, it would be helpful to try testing this again with only one changed variable, so we can nail down the exact variable that decreased conversion.

experiments.title: How adding an acquisition opportunity to a homepage affects conversion rate
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Is a sticky bar or an exit intent more effective in acquiring names on a website?
experiments.summary:
Ended On: 02/20/2018
The Missionary Oblates were wanting to take advantage of the traffic to their website as a channel for email acquisition. To test the most effective way to do this, they tried two options for popups: an exit intent and a sticky bar.
experiments.keyLearnings: Because of the low traffic and conversion rate, this test won’t validate. We recommend putting one of these acquisition opportunities on the website since there currently isn’t an opportunity to turn site traffic into names and potentially donors for the organization.

experiments.title: How the cover artwork for an eBook offer impacts name conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Which eBook cover would be more appealing to people and acquire more names?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/28/2017 - 03/14/2017
The Missionary Oblates set up an acquisition offer for Lent. They were offering people an eBook of Lenten Prayers. The eBook was a resource they already had created, but we hypothesized that something visually brighter and more modern might be more appealing and potentially acquire more names. To test this hypothesis, we developed a new cover for the Lenten Prayers eBook and set up two different Facebook campaigns – one with the original cover that looked more traditional, and one with the new cover that was more modern.
experiments.keyLearnings: While the two covers were drastically different in appearance, there was no signifiant difference in conversion. This experiment didn’t validate, but what it tells us is that the cover of the eBook had no impact on name conversion for the offer. This is great insight to have as we continue to launch new offers using content and artwork that is already developed.

experiments.title: How ordering and numbering the content on the donation page impacts donor conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Would the simplified donation page acquire more donors?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/19/2017 - 04/09/2017
The Missionary Oblates developed an Easter Novena as an acquisition offer. Once a person had signed up for this content offer, they were taken to a donation page. The Missionary Oblates hypothesized that they might be able to increase donations on this page if they simplified the content. They developed a treatment that took the same content as the control and ordered/numbered it on the page. They split the traffic coming to this donation page and tested it.
experiments.keyLearnings: While the test didn’t quite validate, the initial results showed that by simplifying the content on the donation page, we were able to increase donations by 52%. What we can learn from this is that we are able to not only simplify the content on a donation page by ordering/numbering it, but we were able to mentally walk a person through the steps that we wanted them to take on the donation page resulting in more people converting as donors!

experiments.title: How length of copy affects conversion rate in a Facebook ad
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does longer, more in-depth copy about an offer in a Facebook ad increase conversion rate?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 05/18/2017 - 06/07/2017
The Missionary Oblates were running a campaign promoting their new email acquisition offer, a Daily Prayer via email. They wanted to test whether a more in-depth value proposition in the ad copy converted more people than shorter, more concise ad copy. All other elements of the ad remained the same.

experiments.title: How a stepped approach on a donation page affects conversion rate
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Does a stepped approach on a donation page increase donations?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/06/2017 - 06/07/2017
Though this experiment did not validate, there was a 26% lift in conversion rate from the stepped approach treatment copy. The stepped approach allows the Oblates to walk the viewer through the value proposition and why they should give to their mission, which increased clarity in what their gift will accomplish.
experiments.keyLearnings: The Missionary Oblates launched a 9-day Novena offer for Mother’s Day, and wanted to increase conversion on the donation page. They ran a test to see if a stepped approach performed better than paragraphs in the body copy of the page.

experiments.title: How an additional donation ask impacts revenue
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will asking for an additional gift right before checkout increase overall revenue?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 03/13/2017 - 05/19/2017
In an effort to optimize the Oblates online store, we had reached out to the store developers, DonorDirect. In our communication with them, we asked about a variety of features that were on our “wish list” of actions we’d like to be able to take. One of these actions was the ability to turn on an additional ask right before checkout. It turns out that not only was this possible, it was easy to do!
Before moving ahead, we wanted to measure the impact this kind of feature would have on giving. We decided to split test visitors to ensure the additional gift would not have any negative impact on overall revenue.
experiments.keyLearnings:
After running for a full 12 months, we were not able to increase revenue by a statistically significant amount. We did see a directional lift of 16.6% and it did not have any significant impact on the conversion rate. Based upon these results, we have rolled out the feature to the entire audience.
This test taught us to important concepts:
Just because we ask, doesn’t mean people will give. We need to provide a compelling reason to provide an additional gift and we need to make it an easy process to complete.
The motivation of the audience as this stage of checkout is high. The conversion rate of this page is 90% which means we can test out some long, value-heavy copy that may increase the length of the page without having a significant impact on conversion giving the motivation of the audience.

experiments.title: How navigation impact donor conversion rate on landing pages
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will removing navigational elements increase instant donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 06/26/2017 - 07/18/2017
With the launch of the Oblates Celebrating Mercy online course, we wanted to test out some to the more tactical elements of their donation page. Specifically, we had the hypothesis that the navigational elements that were a part of the standard template were causing significant friction to the donation process. We decided to test removing the elements to see if that increased instant donor conversion.
experiments.keyLearnings: After a month an a half of running, we found that removing the navigation did not have any statistically significant impact on donor conversion. When digging deeper into these results, one of the elements that jumped out at us was the overall low instant donor conversion. Based upon the low rate for both the control and treatment, it would seem that we needed to shift our focus away from reducing friction and begin testing into the right value proposition.

experiments.title: How user experience improvements impacted donor conversion
experiments.organization: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
categoryNames: The Missionary Oblates of Mary Immaculate
experiments.researchQuestion: Will an updated user experience increase donor conversion?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 07/18/2017 - 09/26/2017
In an effort to improve the instant donor conversion on the Missionary Oblate’s Celebrating Mercy online course, we wanted to see if we could improve the overall user experience for the donation page. This involved making it mobile-friendly, removing the navigation on the page, and making the text easier to read and follow.
To validate the impact of this change, we set up a test of this new experience vs. the standard store checkout page.
experiments.keyLearnings:
At first glance, it would appear the changes had no statistically significant impact on conversion. However, when we started analyzing the data, we realized this test may have been skewed by some of the technical difficulties experienced during the time period. There was an issue with Facebook that prevented ads from being run for a month and a technical issue that crashed the website for a week.
With the significance of these outside factors, we decided to relaunch this test to see if they had any impact on conversion.
